name: Phil Schaap
description: American jazz disc jockey, historian, and record producer (1951–2021)
abstract: Philip van Noorden Schaap (April 8, 1951 – September 7, 2021) was an American radio host, who specialized in jazz as a broadcaster, historian, archivist, and producer. He hosted an assortment of jazz programs at WKCR and WNYC in New York City and WBGO in Newark, N.J.

interviews.title: Phil Schaap interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: For the Love of Louis

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: Phil Schaap is mentioned only in a parenthetical note by the interviewer/editor; the interviewee Harry Edison does not speak about Phil Schaap.

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Napoleon
about_quotes.evidence: So I took the band — I took the job with the band. That’s how I wound up with his band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Napoleon interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York, New York, January 16, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: just down the street from where I was living was a club called the West End that was run by Phil Schaap... I lived at an apartment at 112th and Riverside Drive that was just around the corner from the West End.
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Avakian George
about_quotes.evidence: I should find out from Phil Schaap where the first saxophonist, lead alto, Earl Warren is, because Earl is out in the Midwest somewhere. He’ll remember, because he was on the team.
about_quotes.doc_title: Avakian George — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jon Weber
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...I said I should get you and Phil Schaap together... [INTERVIEWEE] He’s the Jeopardy champion. He would mop the floor with me. He knows everything.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jon Weber interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 2, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Smulyan
about_quotes.evidence: I listen to Phil Schaap every morning. I mean I’ve been listening to “Bird Flight” for I mean I don’t even know how many years, probably thirty years.
